rationale_for_baseline_tier: Head lice with early localised secondary excoriation — routine treatment and self-care with a clear secondary-infection safety-net; not urgent.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Scalp scratches becoming hot, red, swollen or draining pus
escalation_edges.rationale: Indicates progressing secondary bacterial infection needing antibiotics.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If the scratched areas become hot, red, swollen or start draining pus, get her reviewed promptly.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Fever or redness spreading down the neck
escalation_edges.rationale: Suggests significant secondary infection needing systemic antibiotics.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If she develops a fever or the redness spreads down her neck, seek urgent medical care.

contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Support the parent with a clear step-by-step plan and stress household/contact treatment to prevent reinfestation.
Use age-appropriate products/doses confirmed by the prescriber and involve the parent in application.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: Apply the lice lotion as directed and repeat in 7-10 days, wet-comb daily, keep her nails short, use the anti-itch medicine, treat affected household members, and wash bedding in hot water. Watch the scratches for infection.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: GP in ~7 days; prompt review if the scalp becomes infected or she gets a fever
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: No urgent action needed unless the scalp becomes hot/red/pus-draining or she develops a fever.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Document pediculosis capitis with early impetiginisation, pediculicide + repeat, household treatment, and topical antibiotic.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: No secondary-infection safety-net for an excoriated crusted scalp.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: An impetiginised scalp progresses to cellulitis unnoticed with continued scratching.

serious_under_triage.failure_description: No repeat-treatment/household advice, leading to persistent infestation.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: ED referral for uncomplicated head lice.

clinical_uncertainty_handling.key_uncertainty: Cannot palpate nodes or gauge depth of secondary infection remotely.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.correct_response_to_uncertainty: Treat the lice with a repeat course and household measures, treat crusted areas topically, and safety-net for spreading infection.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.wrong_response_to_uncertainty: Single-treatment/contact-omitting plans, or missing secondary infection.
